.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7979:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:3880:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:33:      "title": "Sole concurrency test exercises the wx fresh-lease path, not the DR-002-01 reclaim re-read CAS",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7980:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:3881:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:34:      "why": "The DR-002-01 fix added a re-read-after-write CAS to the RECLAIM branch of acquireOwnerLeaseFile (mk-code-index-launcher.cjs lines 373-386), which runs only when an `existing` stale owner lease is present. The only concurrency regression test, 'lets exactly one concurrent launcher own the lease' (line 207), spawns two launchers from a fresh createWorkspace() (tmpdir, no seeded owner lease, line 24-42). With no `existing` lease, both launchers take the `writeOwnerLeaseFileExclusive` (O_EXCL `wx`) fresh path (lines 360-371), whose single-winner guarantee comes entirely from O_EXCL and predates DR-002-01. The reclaim-path re-read CAS (the actual code DR-002-01 changed) is never reached: 'reclaims a stale-heartbeat owner lease with a live PID' (line 309) and 'reclaims a dead-pid lease' (line 296) each spawn only ONE launcher against a pre-seeded stale lease, so no two processes ever concurrently hit the reclaim branch. The test therefore passes identically against the un-fixed code (delete lines 377-384 and 'exactly one concurrent launcher' still goes green), giving false confidence that the reclaim CAS is verified.",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7981:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:3882:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/004-code-graph/011-source-bug-and-misalignment-audit/review-r2-opus/deep-review-findings-registry.json:142:      "title": "Owner-lease state machine is duplicated across launcher.cjs and owner-lease.ts with DIVERGENT concurrency primitives",
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7982:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-001.out:4025:.opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/000-release-and-program-cleanup/005-stress-test/003-fix-mcp-runtime-stress-findings/011-research-post-stress-finding-followups/research/deltas/iter-007.jsonl:1:{"type":"iteration","iteration":7,"newInfoRatio":0.34,"status":"completed","focus":"Status snapshot closure and P0/P1 contradiction check","questionsCovered":["Q-P0","Q-P1","Q-P2","Q-OPP","Q-ARCH"],"sourcesRead":[".opencode/skills/sk-deep-research/SKILL.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/SKILL.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/deep-research-strategy.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/iterations/iteration-005.md",".opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/026-graph-and-context-optimization/014-mcp-runtime-stress-remediation/011-post-stress-finding-remediation-research/research/iterations/iteration-006.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/status.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/query.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/handlers/README.md",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/lib/ensure-ready.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/lib/readiness-contract.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/ops/file-watcher.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/code-graph-query-fallback-decision.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/ensure-ready.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/deep-loop/executor-config.vitest.ts",".opencode/skills/system-spec-kit/mcp_server/code_graph/tests/code-graph-siblings-readiness.vitest.ts"],"keyFindings":["Q-P0 winner remains command-owned Copilot target authority because resolveCopilotPromptArg still only chooses raw prompt versus @PROMPT_PATH and has no approved-folder or plan-only Gate 3 contract.","Q-P1 winner remains an isolated SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR degraded-graph harness because unit coverage already exercises all fallbackDecision branches while the stress gap was end-to-end degraded state.","Q-P2 winner is a new non-mutating getGraphReadinessSnapshot helper for code_graph_status; getGraphFreshness currently loses action/reason detail, while ensureCodeGraphReady is unsuitable because it caches, cleans deleted tracked files, and can index.","Do not lower file-watcher debounce first; existing debounce/write-stability/concurrency/dedupe behavior makes status action visibility the lower-risk first fix.","Q-ARCH should carry target-authority vs recovered-context and readiness-action vs freshness-label as the two scoped seams."],"timestamp":"2026-04-27T17:22:17Z"}
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:7983:.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/logs/iter-002.out:114:**COMMAND-DRIVEN FAN-OUT IS SUPPORTED:** use `--executor`/`--executors`/`--concurrency` flags on `/deep:start-review-loop`. The command's YAML `step_fanout_spawn` owns multi-lineage dispatch; `fanout-merge.cjs` applies strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL). This is not ad-hoc shell dispatch — it is the canonical fan-out path. Intra-lineage wave orchestration remains deferred.

.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13123:  1177	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs \
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13148:fanout-merge.cjs consumer merges
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13151:P0, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:284, The research branch reads `deep-research-findings-registry.json`, but the deep-research reducer writes `findings-registry.json` at `.opencode/skills/deep-research/scripts/reduce-state.cjs:904`. Fan-out research can therefore merge the initialized/stale registry and drop the reducer-produced findings. Fix: align the reducer, YAML state path, and fanout merge on one registry filename, or have the merge read the reducer-owned filename with a compatibility fallback.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13153:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:97, Research dedup keys on `finding.id`, but reducer-produced IDs include lineage-local iteration/index data, so the same finding discovered in different lineages will not dedup and `_lineages` attribution is split. Fix: dedup by `contentHash` when present, otherwise normalized `text`, and merge sources/lineages.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13155:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:174, Review dedup keys on lineage-local `findingId`/`title`, ignoring the reducer’s content-hash contract and fallback key (`contentHash`, else file:line + title). Different findings can collide on common local IDs like `F001`, while the same finding with different local IDs duplicates. Fix: reuse the reducer dedup key and merge dimensions, merged IDs, evidence, and lineage attribution.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13157:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:65, Malformed JSON is swallowed as `null`, and lineages with null registries are skipped at line 305. A review lineage containing an active P0 but a malformed registry can be omitted, allowing a merged PASS/CONDITIONAL despite the strongest-restriction contract. Fix: distinguish missing from parse-failed registries and fail closed on parse errors.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13164:[{"id":"f-p123-i3-01","severity":"P0","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":284,"issue":"The research branch reads deep-research-findings-registry.json, but the deep-research reducer writes findings-registry.json, so fan-out research can merge stale or empty registries and drop reducer-produced findings.","fix":"Align the reducer, YAML state path, and fanout merge on one registry filename, or have fanout-merge read the reducer-owned filename with a compatibility fallback."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-02","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":97,"issue":"Research dedup keys on finding.id, whose reducer-produced value is lineage-local, so identical findings from different lineages do not dedup and attribution is split.","fix":"Dedup by contentHash when present, otherwise normalized text, and merge sources plus lineage attribution."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":174,"issue":"Review dedup keys on lineage-local findingId/title instead of the reducer content-hash contract, causing unrelated local IDs to collide and same findings with different local IDs to duplicate.","fix":"Use contentHash primary and file:line plus normalized title fallback, preserving highest severity while merging dimensions, merged IDs, evidence, and lineages."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-04","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":65,"issue":"Malformed registry JSON is swallowed as null and later skipped, so a lineage with an active P0 can be omitted from the merged review verdict.","fix":"Return a distinct parse-failure state and fail closed on malformed lineage registries instead of counting them as skipped."}]
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13171:fanout-merge.cjs consumer merges

Fan-out research can therefore merge the initialized/stale registry and drop the reducer-produced findings. Fix: align the reducer, YAML state path, and fanout merge on one registry filename, or have the merge read the reducer-owned filename with a compatibility fallback.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13176:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:97, Research dedup keys on `finding.id`, but reducer-produced IDs include lineage-local iteration/index data, so the same finding discovered in different lineages will not dedup and `_lineages` attribution is split. Fix: dedup by `contentHash` when present, otherwise normalized `text`, and merge sources/lineages.

Different findings can collide on common local IDs like `F001`, while the same finding with different local IDs duplicates. Fix: reuse the reducer dedup key and merge dimensions, merged IDs, evidence, and lineage attribution.
.opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13180:P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs:65, Malformed JSON is swallowed as `null`, and lineages with null registries are skipped at line 305. .opencode/specs/skilled-agent-orchestration/123-deep-loop-parallel-fanout/008-deep-review/review/iterations/iteration-003.md:13187:[{"id":"f-p123-i3-01","severity":"P0","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":284,"issue":"The research branch reads deep-research-findings-registry.json, but the deep-research reducer writes findings-registry.json, so fan-out research can merge stale or empty registries and drop reducer-produced findings.","fix":"Align the reducer, YAML state path, and fanout merge on one registry filename, or have fanout-merge read the reducer-owned filename with a compatibility fallback."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-02","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":97,"issue":"Research dedup keys on finding.id, whose reducer-produced value is lineage-local, so identical findings from different lineages do not dedup and attribution is split.","fix":"Dedup by contentHash when present, otherwise normalized text, and merge sources plus lineage attribution."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-03","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":174,"issue":"Review dedup keys on lineage-local findingId/title instead of the reducer content-hash contract, causing unrelated local IDs to collide and same findings with different local IDs to duplicate.","fix":"Use contentHash primary and file:line plus normalized title fallback, preserving highest severity while merging dimensions, merged IDs, evidence, and lineages."},{"id":"f-p123-i3-04","severity":"P1","file":".opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-merge.cjs","line":65,"issue":"Malformed registry JSON is swallowed as null and later skipped, so a lineage with an active P0 can be omitted from the merged review verdict.","fix":"Return a distinct parse-failure state and fail closed on malformed lineage registries instead of counting them as skipped."}]

    99	|-------|----------|--------------|---------|------------------|
   100	| `review_target` | Y | `$ARGUMENTS` first positional target, or marker `review_target` | none | N |
   101	| `review_target_type` | Y | marker `review_target_type`, or auto-detect from `review_target` | inferred only | Y, when target is present but ambiguous |
   102	| `review_dimensions` | Y | flag `--dims` if supported by caller, marker `review_dimensions`, or default | `"all"` | N |
   103	| `spec_folder` | Y | flag `--spec-folder`, marker `spec_folder`, or target path when target is a spec folder | none | Y, when target is not a spec folder |
   104	| `execution_mode` | Y | attached suffix `:auto` or marker `execution_mode` | `AUTONOMOUS` under `:auto` | N |
   105	| `maxIterations` | Y | flag `--max-iterations`, marker `maxIterations`, or default | `7` | N |
   106	| `convergenceThreshold` | Y | flag `--convergence`, marker `convergenceThreshold`, or default | `0.10` | N |
   107	| `executor` | N | flag `--executor`, marker `executor`, config file, or default | `native` | N |
   108	| `executor_model` | N | flag `--model`, marker `executor_model`, or executor-specific validation | none | N |
   109	| `executor_reasoning` | N | flag `--reasoning-effort`, marker `executor_reasoning`, or executor default | none | N |
   110	| `executor_service_tier` | N | flag `--service-tier`, marker `executor_service_tier`, or executor default | none | N |
   111	| `executor_timeout` | N | flag `--executor-timeout`, marker `executor_timeout`, or default | `900` | N |
   112	| `resource_map_emit` | N | flag `--no-resource-map`, marker `resource_map_emit`, or default | `true` | N |
   113	| `fanout_executors` | N | repeatable `--executor=<type>` flags or `--executors=<json>`; each group accepts `--model`, `--reasoning-effort`, `--service-tier`, `--executor-timeout`, `--iters`, `--label`, `--count` | none (single-executor when absent) | N |
   114	| `fanout_concurrency` | N | flag `--concurrency=N` | `2` | N |
   115	
   116	**Fan-out default policy:** 0–1 `--executor` flags and no `--executors` → `config.executor` (single-executor, default, unchanged). 2+ `--executor` flags, `--executors`, or any `--count > 1` → `config.fanout`. Review fan-out uses strongest-restriction: any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL. Native fan-out (count N for `native` executor) runs N sequential `@deep-review` sub-agents; CLI fan-out runs N headless subprocesses in the capped pool.
   117	
   118	### Consolidated Setup Prompt for `:confirm` and No-Suffix Mode
   119	
   120	Use this block only when `execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"` or when no suffix was supplied and Q2 must ask for the execution mode.
   121	
   122	**STATUS: BLOCKED**
   123	
   124	```
   125	EXECUTE THIS SINGLE CONSOLIDATED PROMPT:
   126	
   127	1. CHECK for mode suffix:
   128	   |-- ":auto"    -> execution_mode = "AUTONOMOUS"
   129	   |-- ":confirm" -> execution_mode = "INTERACTIVE"
   130	   +-- No suffix  -> execution_mode = "ASK" (include Q2)
   131	
   132	2. CHECK $ARGUMENTS for target (review_target):
   133	   |-- Has content (ignoring suffixes and flags):
   134	   |     -> review_target = $ARGUMENTS, omit Q0
   135	   +-- Empty -> include Q0
   136	
   137	3. PARSE optional flags from $ARGUMENTS:
   138	   |-- --max-iterations=N -> maxIterations = N
   139	   |-- --convergence=N -> convergenceThreshold = N
   140	   |-- --spec-folder=PATH -> spec_path = PATH, omit Q1
   141	   |-- --executor=<type> -> config.executor.type (`native` | `cli-codex` | `cli-gemini` | `cli-claude-code` | `cli-opencode` | `cli-devin`)
   142	   |-- --model=<id> -> config.executor.model (for example `gpt-5.4`)
   143	   |-- --reasoning-effort=<level> -> config.executor.reasoningEffort (`none` | `minimal` | `low` | `medium` | `high` | `xhigh`)
   144	   |-- --service-tier=<tier> -> config.executor.serviceTier (`priority` | `standard` | `fast`)
   145	   |-- --executor-timeout=<seconds> -> config.executor.timeoutSeconds (positive integer, default `900`)
   146	   |-- --no-resource-map -> config.resource_map.emit = false
   147	   |-- --executor=<type> [--model=X] [--reasoning-effort=Y] [--service-tier=Z] [--executor-timeout=N] [--iters=N] [--label=X] [--count=N]
   148	   |     (repeatable; each occurrence adds one entry to config.fanout.executors)
   149	   |-- --executors=<json> -> config.fanout.executors = parse(json) escape hatch
   150	   |-- --concurrency=N -> config.fanout.concurrency (default 2)
   151	   |
   152	   |   Fan-out default policy:
   153	   |   - 0 or 1 --executor (no --executors, no count>1): write config.executor (single, unchanged)
   154	   |   - 2+ --executor flags, --executors, or any count>1: write config.fanout
   155	   |   - Review fan-out verdict: strongest-restriction (any lineage active P0 → merged FAIL)
   156	   |
   157	   +-- Defaults: maxIterations=7, convergenceThreshold=0.10, config.executor.type=`native`, config.executor.timeoutSeconds=900, config.resource_map.emit=`true`
   158	
   159	   Executor precedence for setup resolution:
   160	   - CLI flag > config file > schema defaults

    21	<!-- SPECKIT_TEMPLATE_SOURCE: spec-core | v2.2 -->
    22	<!-- SPECKIT_LEVEL: 2 -->
    23	
    24	# Phase 001 — Fan-out schema + config plumbing
    25	
    26	## Purpose
    27	Add the opt-in multi-executor config representation without changing any behavior. Foundation for all later phases.
    28	
    29	## Scope
    30	- `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`: add `lineageExecutorSchema` (= `executorConfigSchema` + dir-safe `label`, `count`, nullable `iterations`), `fanoutConfigSchema` (`executors[]`, `concurrency` default 2), `parseFanoutConfig` (delegates per-entry to existing `parseExecutorConfig`), `expandLineages` (count→labels). Do NOT modify `executorConfigSchema`/`parseExecutorConfig`.
    31	- Backward-compat: config carries EITHER `config.executor` OR `config.fanout`, never both; both-present fails fast.
    32	- `deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts`: optional `lineageId` on `buildExecutorAuditRecord` + `RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput` (absent ⇒ unchanged records).
    33	
    34	## Success
    35	- New unit tests cover fan-out parse / both-present conflict / count-expansion / per-entry kind validation reuse.
    36	- **Parity gate:** existing single-executor `parseExecutorConfig` tests byte-identical; full vitest green.
    37	
    38	## Out of scope
    39	Pool, spawn, merge, YAML, docs (later phases).

 succeeded in 0ms:
     1	---
     2	title: "Fan-out config schema"
     3	description: "Multi-executor fan-out config layer: lineageExecutorSchema, fanoutConfigSchema, parseFanoutConfig, expandLineages — sits on top of the existing single-executor config without modifying it."
     4	---
     5	
     6	# Fan-out config schema
     7	
     8	---
     9	
    10	## 1. OVERVIEW
    11	
    12	Adds the opt-in multi-executor config representation above the existing `executorConfigSchema`.
    13	Defines `lineageExecutorSchema` (executorConfigSchema extended with a dir-safe `label`
    14	matching `/^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/`, `count` int≥1 default 1, `iterations` int≥1|null default
    15	null), `fanoutConfigSchema` (`executors[]` min 1, `concurrency` int≥1 default 2),
    16	`parseFanoutConfig` (delegates each entry's executor subset to the existing
    17	`parseExecutorConfig` — reuses all kind/model/flag rules — then enforces unique labels and
    18	collision-safe count expansion), and `expandLineages` (count=1 keeps base label; count>1
    19	yields `label-1…label-N`, each with count:1).
    20	
    21	### Why This Matters
    22	
    23	Foundation for all fan-out dispatch. If this schema drifts, all downstream pool, CLI driver,
    24	salvage, and merge primitives will receive malformed lineage descriptors.
    25	
    26	---
    27	
    28	## 2. CURRENT REALITY
    29	
    30	Fully shipped in `executor-config.ts`. `parseFanoutConfig` validates uniqueness of declared
    31	labels AND of expanded labels (e.g. a base label `x` with count:3 must not collide with
    32	another declared label `x-2`). The optional `lineageId` field on
    33	`RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput` and `buildExecutorAuditRecord` is a companion addition —
    34	conditionally spread so records are byte-identical when absent (parity guaranteed).
    35	
    36	---
    37	
    38	## 3. SOURCE FILES
    39	
    40	### Implementation
    41	
    42	| File | Role |
    43	|---|---|
    44	| `lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts` | `lineageExecutorSchema` (~L294), `fanoutConfigSchema` (~L304), `parseFanoutConfig` (~L323), `expandLineages` (~L381) |
    45	| `lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts` | Optional `lineageId` on `RunAuditedExecutorCommandInput` (~L106) and `buildExecutorAuditRecord` (~L491); conditional spread preserves byte-identical single-executor records |
    46	
    47	### Validation
    48	
    49	| File | Role |
    50	|---|---|
    51	| `tests/unit/executor-config.vitest.ts` | 9 fan-out tests added to the existing 27: happy path, unique-label enforcement, expanded-label collision detection, count expansion (label-1…label-N), per-entry kind validation reuse (cli-codex requires model) |
    52	
    53	---
    54	
    55	## 4. SOURCE METADATA
    56	
    57	- Group: Fan-Out
    58	- Feature ID: F023
    59	- Catalog source: `feature_catalog/09--fanout/01-fanout-config-schema.md`
    60	- Primary source files: `lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts`, `lib/deep-loop/executor-audit.ts`

   214	describe('parseFanoutConfig', () => {
   215	  it('accepts a multi-executor fan-out config with defaults', () => {
   216	    const config = parseFanoutConfig({
   217	      executors: [
   218	        { kind: 'native', label: 'opus' },
   219	        { kind: 'cli-codex', model: 'gpt-5.4', label: 'gpt' },
   220	      ],
   221	    });
   222	    expect(config.executors).toHaveLength(2);
   223	    expect(config.concurrency).toBe(2);
   224	    expect(config.executors[0].count).toBe(1);
   225	    expect(config.executors[0].iterations).toBeNull();
   226	  });
   227	
   228	  it('honors explicit concurrency, count, and per-lineage iterations', () => {
   229	    const config = parseFanoutConfig({
   230	      concurrency: 3,
   231	      executors: [{ kind: 'native', label: 'opus', count: 5, iterations: 5 }],
   232	    });
   233	    expect(config.concurrency).toBe(3);
   234	    expect(config.executors[0].count).toBe(5);
   235	    expect(config.executors[0].iterations).toBe(5);
   236	  });
   237	
   238	  it('reuses per-executor kind validation (cli-codex requires model)', () => {
   239	    expect(() => parseFanoutConfig({ executors: [{ kind: 'cli-codex', label: 'gpt' }] })).toThrow(ExecutorConfigError);
   240	  });
   241	
   242	  it('reuses per-executor flag-support validation (cli-gemini rejects reasoningEffort)', () => {
   243	    expect(() =>
   244	      parseFanoutConfig({ executors: [{ kind: 'cli-gemini', model: 'gemini-3.1-pro-preview', reasoningEffort: 'high', label: 'gem' }] }),
   245	    ).toThrow(ExecutorConfigError);
   246	  });
   247	
   248	  it('rejects an empty executors array', () => {
   249	    expect(() => parseFanoutConfig({ executors: [] })).toThrow(ExecutorConfigError);
   250	  });
   251	
   252	  it('rejects a label that is not dir-safe', () => {
   253	    expect(() => parseFanoutConfig({ executors: [{ kind: 'native', label: 'Bad Label' }] })).toThrow(ExecutorConfigError);
   254	  });
   255	
   256	  it('rejects duplicate lineage labels', () => {
   257	    expect(() =>
   258	      parseFanoutConfig({ executors: [{ kind: 'native', label: 'a' }, { kind: 'native', label: 'a' }] }),
   259	    ).toThrow(ExecutorConfigError);
   260	  });
   261	});
   262	
   263	describe('expandLineages', () => {
   264	  it('keeps the base label when count is 1', () => {
   265	    const config = parseFanoutConfig({ executors: [{ kind: 'native', label: 'opus', count: 1 }] });
   266	    expect(expandLineages(config).map((l) => l.label)).toEqual(['opus']);
   267	  });
   268	
   269	  it('expands count>1 into numbered single-replica lineages', () => {
   270	    const config = parseFanoutConfig({
   271	      executors: [
   272	        { kind: 'cli-opencode', model: 'minimax/MiniMax-M2.7', label: 'minimax', count: 3 },
   273	        { kind: 'native', label: 'opus', count: 2 },
   274	      ],
   275	    });
   276	    const lineages = expandLineages(config);
   277	    expect(lineages.map((l) => l.label)).toEqual(['minimax-1', 'minimax-2', 'minimax-3', 'opus-1', 'opus-2']);

   279	  });
   280	});

   294	export const lineageExecutorSchema = executorConfigSchema.extend({
   295	  label: z.string().min(1).regex(LINEAGE_LABEL_PATTERN, {
   296	    message: "label must match /^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9-]*$/ (lowercase, digits, hyphens; dir-safe)",
   297	  }),
   298	  count: z.number().int().positive().default(1),
   299	  iterations: z.number().int().positive().nullable().default(null),
   300	});
   301	
   302	export type LineageExecutor = z.infer<typeof lineageExecutorSchema>;
   303	
   304	export const fanoutConfigSchema = z.object({
   305	  executors: z.array(lineageExecutorSchema).min(1),
   306	  concurrency: z.number().int().positive().default(2),
   307	});
   308	
   309	export type FanoutConfig = z.infer<typeof fanoutConfigSchema>;
   310	
   311	/**
   312	 * Parse and validate a raw fan-out configuration.
   313	 *
   314	 * Each entry's executor subset is routed through {@link parseExecutorConfig} so
   315	 * ALL existing kind/model/flag rules (cli-codex needs model, gemini/devin model
   316	 * whitelists, per-kind flag support) apply per lineage with zero duplication.
   317	 * Labels must be unique and base-label (count>1) expansion must not collide.

   323	export function parseFanoutConfig(raw: unknown): FanoutConfig {

   326	    throw new ExecutorConfigError({ issues: parsed.error.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path, message: issue.message })) });
   327	  }
   328	
   329	  const config = parsed.data;

   331	  // Reuse the canonical single-executor validator per entry (kind/model/flags).

   333	    const { label: _label, count: _count, iterations: _iterations, ...executorSubset } = entry;

   335	      parseExecutorConfig(executorSubset);

   681	        action: "H-2 loop-prevention guard: scan previous iteration's rendered prompt for nested review marker"
   682	        algorithm: |

   684	            PREV_PROMPT_PATH = "{state_paths.prompt_dir}/iteration-{previous_iteration}.md"
   685	            if file_exists(PREV_PROMPT_PATH):

   687	              if PREV_PROMPT_FIRST_LINE matches ^(DEEP-REVIEW|DEEP-RESEARCH|CODE-REVIEW)$:

   689	                halt with exit code 2

 succeeded in 0ms:
   280	          skill: |
   281	            1. Read SKILL.md from .opencode/skills/{skill_name}/

   284	            4. Find command entry points referencing this skill

   286	          agent: |

   289	            3. Set scope = all discovered agent files
   290	          track: |
   291	            1. List all child spec folders under specs/{track}/ or .opencode/specs/{track}/

   293	            3. Set scope = all child spec artifacts
   294	          files: |

   299	        outputs:
   300	          - review_scope_files: "List of all files in review scope"
   301	          - cross_reference_targets: "{ spec_paths: [...], code_paths: [...], test_paths: [...] }"
   302	
   303	      step_order_dimensions:
   304	        action: "Order review dimensions based on risk priority"
   305	        algorithm: |
   306	          1. Iteration 0 (inventory pass): Build artifact map, identify file types, estimate complexity
   307	          2. Subsequent iterations follow risk-ordered deep passes:
   308	             correctness → security → traceability → maintainability
   309	          3. If review_dimensions != "all", filter to only requested dimensions
   310	          4. Derive traceability protocol plan:
   311	             - core: spec_code, checklist_evidence
   312	             - overlay: skill_agent, agent_cross_runtime, feature_catalog_code, playbook_capability
   313	          5. If resource_map_present, reserve at least one traceability-oriented pass to cross-check target_files from applied/T-*.md against resource-map.md and classify touched / expected-by-scope / gap / absent-from-map outcomes
   314	          6. Store ordered list as dimension_queue
   315	        outputs:
   316	          - dimension_queue: "Ordered list of dimensions to review"
   317	          - inventory_pass: true
   318	
   319	      step_normalize_review_dimensions:
   320	        action: "Normalize reviewDimensions once during init for config + JSONL reuse"
   321	        outputs:
   322	          - review_dimensions_normalized: "Canonical string[] for deep-review-config.json"
   323	          - review_dimensions_json: "JSON.stringify(review_dimensions_normalized) for JSONL config record"
   324	        normalize: |
   325	          Accept {review_dimensions} in any of these shapes and convert once:
   326	            - "all" → ["correctness","security","traceability","maintainability"]
   327	            - "correctness,security" → ["correctness","security"]
   328	            - ["correctness"] → ["correctness"]
   329	          Every element MUST be one of the four canonical dimension names.
   330	
   331	      step_create_config:
   332	        action: "Create deep-review-config.json from template with review-specific fields"
   333	        template: .opencode/skills/deep-review/assets/deep_review_config.json
   334	        populate:

   338	          parentSessionId: null
   339	          lineageMode: "new"
   340	          generation: 1
   341	          continuedFromRun: null
   342	          reviewTarget: "{review_target}"
   343	          reviewTargetType: "{review_target_type}"
   344	          # Reuse the single normalized init-time value from step_normalize_review_dimensions.

   349	          crossReference:

   251	          append_jsonl: '{"type":"event","event":"resumed","mode":"review","sessionId":"{session_id}","parentSessionId":"{session_id}","lineageMode":"resume","continuedFromRun":{iteration_count},"generation":{generation},"archivedPath":null,"timestamp":"{ISO_8601_NOW}"}'
   252	          skip_to: phase_loop

   254	          action: "Archive the current review packet under the root-level review_archive/ tree and start a new lineage segment with a fresh sessionId and generation+1."

 succeeded in 0ms:
   128	        - artifact_root_dir: "Resolved local review owner directory"
   129	        - artifact_subfolder: "Local packet subfolder ({ownerSlug}-pt-{NN}), else null"
   130	        - artifact_dir: "Resolved review packet directory for this target"
   131	        - artifact_archive_root: "Resolved local review archive directory for this target"
   132	
   133	  step_fanout_spawn:
   134	    description: "Spawn fan-out lineages; skip and proceed to phase_init normally when single-executor"
   135	    skip_when: "config.fanout is absent"
   136	    instructions: |
   137	      Fan-out mode is active. Spawn all lineages. Each lineage runs the full loop in its own
   138	      isolated sub-packet under {artifact_dir}/lineages/{label}/.
   139	      After all lineages complete, jump directly to phase_synthesis — skip phase_init and
   140	      phase_main_loop entirely (those are the single-executor path). The merge step in
   141	      phase_synthesis will consolidate results across all lineages.
   142	    sub_steps:
   143	      step_fanout_spawn_cli:
   144	        description: "Spawn all CLI lineages in capped worker pool"
   145	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind != 'native'"
   146	        command: |
   147	          node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/scripts/fanout-run.cjs \

   149	            --loop-type review \
   150	            --fanout-config-json '{config.fanout_json}' \
   151	            --base-artifact-dir {artifact_dir}
   152	        working_directory: "{repo_root}"
   153	        wait_for_completion: true
   154	      step_fanout_spawn_native:

   156	        skip_when: "no entries in config.fanout.executors with kind == 'native'"

   158	        dispatch:

   164	              config.fanout_lineage_artifact_dir: {artifact_dir}/lineages/{lineage.label}
   165	              session_id: fanout-{lineage.label}-{fanout_run_id}
   166	              executor: native
   167	            Run to convergence. Write all outputs to the override directory.

codex
## Focus
executor-config.ts schema

## Findings
P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts:304, `fanoutConfigSchema` only models the fanout block and Zod object parsing strips extra top-level keys, so a raw config carrying both `executor` and `executors`/fanout data is accepted instead of failing the both-present conflict contract. Fix: add an explicit root-level executor-vs-fanout parser/guard, or make this parser strict and reject `executor` when present.

P1, .opencode/skills/deep-loop-runtime/lib/deep-loop/executor-config.ts:324, `parseFanoutConfig` parses each lineage through `lineageExecutorSchema` before delegating to `parseExecutorConfig`, so legacy `type` is stripped and never reaches the normalizer/conflict detector; `type: "cli-codex"` without `kind` can become default `native`. Fix: preprocess each raw lineage with the same legacy normalization before schema parsing, or make the lineage schema accept and reconcile `type` before stripping unknown keys.

## Verdict
Fanout validation mostly covers kind/model/default/label expansion, but the conflict/legacy compatibility edge cases are incomplete.
Review verdict: CONDITIONAL

===FINDINGS_JSON===

===END_FINDINGS===
